surface: NON-MATH: letter-counting ('how many letters in W?'), verifier-checkable (len), decontaminated (54 train / 30 eval word-disjoint), per-INSTANCE confidence target (empirical accuracy as a continuous function of word length)
purpose: rule out the 'math-taxonomy-specific' concern for the W2 calibration-SFT result
verdict: DIRECTIONALLY SUPPORTIVE, NOT A CLEAN PROMOTION -> w2 stays OPEN. On letter-counting the calibration-SFT lowers held-out ECE (base 0.533 -> 0.43/0.458/0.458) and improves accuracy-at-coverage (0.40 -> 1.0 all seeds). BUT: (1) the per-seed ΔECE 95% CIs INCLUDE 0 (N=30 held-out is underpowered); (2) a CAPABILITY CONFOUND -- the SFT taught the correct answers so accuracy JUMPED 0.467 -> 0.80/0.83/0.83, so the ECE change mixes calibration with a capability gain (unlike the math surface where base accuracy was already high and only confidence changed). So the non-math surface supports the DIRECTION but does not cleanly replicate the math-surface result. The strongest W2 evidence remains the math surface (3 seeds, all ΔECE CIs exclude 0).
nextToClose: A larger non-math held-out (N>=150) AND an accuracy-controlled design (SFT that changes only stated confidence, not the answer, on a surface where base accuracy is already moderate) so ECE reduction is isolated from capability gain.
